commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=afdfdef48e842b9ed62e5481bd9d96fb8bd63bbb)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes memory-wiki unsafe-local sync to report unreadable configured sources, skip imported-source pruning while any source is unreadable, and adds regression tests for top-level and nested source outages plus readable-directory deletion.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +82. Total +116 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-backed. The linked issue includes a current-main production-function repro, and source inspection confirms current main still drops missing configured paths before the shared prune deletes inactive unsafe-local entries.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed because the branch still lacks contributor real behavior proof and changes unsafe-local prune compatibility in a way automation should not merge without scope approval.

target.title: fix : don't prune unsafe local pages when a source path is transiently unavailable
target.body:
Closes #97523

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where memory-wiki users running `vaultMode: "unsafe-local"` would permanently lose imported pages **and their hand-written human-notes blocks** when a configured source path became transiently unavailable (an undocked external drive, a rebooting NAS, or a cloud-synced folder that is not mounted yet). Any `wiki_*` tool that triggered a sync while the path was gone deleted every imported page for that path; when the path came back, pages were recreated empty and the user's notes were unrecoverable.

## Why This Change Was Made

A transiently unreadable source collects zero artifacts in exactly the same way a real deletion does, and the `unsafe-local` sync pruned unconditionally on that signal. `collectUnsafeLocalArtifacts` now reports when a configured path could not be read — either `fs.stat` failing on the path itself, or `fs.readdir` failing on a present-but-unreadable directory — and `syncMemoryWikiUnsafeLocalSources` skips `pruneImportedSourceEntries` whenever that happens, so nothing is deleted until every configured source is readable again. This mirrors the existing capability-gated prune guard already used by the bridge-sync path (#68373). Genuine deletions (a file removed from a directory that is still readable) continue to prune as before.

## User Impact

Users with `unsafe-local` notes on removable, network, or cloud-synced storage no longer lose imported pages or their manually authored human-notes when a source path is briefly offline. After the source returns, the next sync reconciles cleanly with notes intact. No config changes required.

## Evidence

- Added a regression test that imports a page, writes a user note into its human-notes block, takes the source directory offline (real `fs.rename` outage), syncs, and asserts the page and the note both survive (removedCount 0); after re-mount the next sync stays clean and the note is still present.
- Reworked the existing prune test to delete a file inside a still-readable configured directory, confirming genuine deletions still prune (removedCount 1).
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-wiki/src/unsafe-local.test.ts` -> 4 passed.
- oxlint and `oxfmt --check` clean on both changed files.

Note: the local full extension lane shows 4 failures in source-sync-state.test.ts / doctor-contract-api.test.ts with `TypeError: statement.columns is not a function`. These are pre-existing and reproduce identically on a clean checkout with my changes stashed; they are a node:sqlite incompatibility with local Node 23 (non-LTS) and are unrelated to this change. CI runs on Linux Node 24.

AI-assisted (Claude Code); change reviewed and understood by the author.
target.author: lexes7
